items.description: iOS: NSAllowsArbitraryLoads is false — ATS strictly enforced
Android: android:debuggable=false in release build manifest
Android: usesCleartextTraffic=false — TLS enforced for all network traffic
Certificate pinning implemented for high-value API calls
Sensitive data not stored in SharedPreferences, external storage, or plist in plaintext
Secrets stored in iOS Keychain / Android Keystore — not in code or config files
Biometric authentication properly tied to Keychain/Keystore — not bypassable
Screenshot prevention enabled for sensitive screens (payment, auth)
Sensitive fields (passwords, card numbers) block clipboard access
Android Network Security Config restricts cleartext and pins certificates
Release build uses code obfuscation (ProGuard/R8 for Android, Swift symbol stripping for iOS)
Root/jailbreak detection implemented for high-risk operations
Deep links validated — no open redirect or intent injection via deep link handling
No API keys, secrets, or credentials embedded in app binary or resources
OWASP MASVS L2 checklist completed for release build
Mobile dependencies scanned for known CVEs
Threat model completed and reviewed for this mobile surface change
Data residency requirements met — no user data stored on device beyond session
allowBackup=false in Android manifest — sensitive data not included in backups
No sensitive data logged in production builds — crash reporting sanitized
Android tapjacking prevention: FLAG_SECURE set on sensitive screens; filterTouchesWhenObscured=true on clickable elements handling auth or payments
Sensitive data (passwords, keys, PAN) explicitly zeroed from memory after use — not relying on GC; no sensitive data in String objects (use char[] or SecureString)
Anti-debugging and anti-tampering controls in place for high-risk operations; ptrace detection and integrity attestation verified before sensitive operations
